data.title: Polestar’s Q1 retail sales up 76% Y/Y
data.description:
Polestar (NASDAQ:PSNY) retail sales volumes amounted to an estimated 12,304 cars in the first quarter of 2025, up 76% compared to the same quarter last year and stable versus Q4 2024.

Sales are supported by a growing uptake of newer models and the ongoing transition to an active selling model, the Swedish electric vehicle maker said.

Polestar expects to publish its full-year results for 2024 and file its annual report on Form 20-F, by the end of April 2025.

Shares fell 4.1% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Canaan reports increased bitcoin mining in March
data.description:
• Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) mined 90 bitcoins (BTC-USD) in March, representing an increase of 9.8% compared to February.
  • The firm's month-end operating hashrate stood at 5.97 EH/s, while its average all-in power cost came in at $0.043/kWh.
  • The company also expanded its mining operations in North America, adding 0.32 exahash per second (EH/s) of installed computing power.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Virtus Investment Partners estimates lower AUM as of March end
data.description:
Virtus Investment Partners (NYSE:VRTS) announced on Thursday preliminary AUM of $167.46B as of March 31, down from $174.53B at February-end and $175.00 at the end of December 2024.

The decrease was primarily attributed to market performance and net outflows in U.S. retail mutual funds, institutional accounts and retail separate accounts.

By product type, Open-End Funds had AUM of $53.61B at the end of March, Closed-End Funds of $10.27B, Retail Separate Accounts of $46.92B, and Institutional Accounts of $56.66B.

data.title: Climb Channel Solutions partners with cybersecurity company
Piedmont Lithium to become Elevra Lithium upon merger completion with Sayona Mining
data.description:
Piedmont Lithium (NASDAQ:PLL) said Thursday it will rebrand as Elevra Lithium following the completion of its planned merger with Sayona Mining (OTCQB:SYAXF), which was announced in November.

Piedmont (NASDAQ:PLL) and Sayona (OTCQB:SYAXF) have selected their respective nominees to Elevra Lithium's board, which will consist of four from Piedmont's current board - including Dawne Hickton, the Chair designate of Elevra Lithium - and four from Sayona's board - including Lucas Dow, who will become Elevra's Managing Director and CEO.

Current Piedmont (PLL) CEO Keith Phillips will retire and become a strategic advisor to Elevra Lithium for a transition period.

The companies said the transaction is targeted to close in mid-2025 and remains subject to approval by Piedmont (PLL) and Sayona (OTCQB:SYAXF) shareholders, as well as regulatory approvals.

data.title: EU looks to pause countermeasures against U.S. tariffs
data.description:
The European Commission said it will pause the proposed tariff countermeasures after U.S. President Donald Trump said he was issuing a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs.

"We want to give negotiations a chance," European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said.

Trump on Wednesday said he was issuing a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries that had reached out for negotiations. He also slapped China with a higher rate.

EU's plans to pause the potential countermeasures come a couple of days after the European Commission proposed imposing 25% counter-tariffs on a range of U.S. goods in response to Trump's tariffs on steel and aluminum.

"While finalising the adoption of the EU countermeasures that saw strong support from our Member States, we will put them on hold for 90 days. If negotiations are not satisfactory, our countermeasures will kick in," Leyen said in a post on social media platform X.

Leyen, however, added that the preparatory work on further countermeasures will continue, and "all options remain on the table."

Europe-related ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Buckle’s comparable sales up 3.7% Y/Y in March
data.description:
• The Buckle’s (NYSE:BKE) comparable store net sales grew 3.7% year-over-year for the 5-week period ended April 5, 2025.
  • March net sales increased 4.5% Y/Y to $109.1M. Year-to-date, comparable store net sales were up 1.6% Y/Y, with net sales up 2.2% Y/Y to $186.6M.

data.title: EU pauses countermeasures against US tariffs for 90 days
data.description:
European Commission President Thursday said the EU will put tariff retaliation on hold for 90 days to match Donald Trump's pause.

"While finalising the adoption of the EU countermeasures that saw strong support from our Member States, we will put them on hold for 90 days," Ursula von der Leyen said in a post on social media platform X.

"If negotiations are not satisfactory, our countermeasures will kick in. Preparatory work on further countermeasures continues. As I have said before, all options remain on the table," she added.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).
